LAW AND MOTION 302,RESPONDENT ELISABETH THERIOT'S MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT FILED APRIL 26, 2016 IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY PER CCP 663A. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT PETITIONER FARELLA BRAUN + MARTEL, LLP DID NOT SERVE A REQUIRED NOTICE OF ARBITRATION AS CONTENDED BY MS. THERIOT, THAT IS NOT A BASIS TO AVOID THE STRICT TIME LIMITS TO SEEK TO VACATE A JUDGMENT PER CCP 663A. THE COURT'S RECORDS REFLECT THAT WITHIN THE TIME FRAME PERMITTED TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT MS. THERIOT REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL FILED BOTH AN APPLICATION TO STAY EXECUTION OF THE JUDGMENT AND A MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE JUDGMENT, SHOWING THAT SHE HAD THE ABILITY TO AND COULD RAISE ALL ARGUMENTS THAT SHE BELIEVED APPROPRIATE WITHIN THE TIME FRAME OF SECTION 663A. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD AND RESULTING JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF PETITIONER (TRANSACTION ID # 100000288) FILED BY RESPONDENT THIERIOT, ELISABETH L HEARING SET FOR AUG-09-2016 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-09-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER AND JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT ELISABETH THIERIOT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD AND RESULTING JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF PETITIONER. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED FOR EACH OF THREE INDEPENDENTLY SUFFICIENT REASONS. FIRST, THE CCP 1288.2 100-DAY DEADLINE FOR MOVING TO VACATE AN ARBITRATION AWARD IS MANDATORY AND JURISDICTIONAL, SO CCP 473(B) RELIEF IS NOT AVAILABLE. (ABERS V. ROHRS (2013) 217 CAL.APP.4TH 1199, 1211-12.) SECOND, THEIRIOT DID NOT RETAIN THE COUNSEL WHO SUPPOSEDLY ERRED UNTIL AFTER THE 100-DAY DEADLINE HAD PASSED, SO HIS SUPPOSED ERROR DID NOT CAUSE THE DEADLINE TO BE MISSED. THIRD, CCP 473(B)(2) - UNDER WHICH THEIRIOT MOVES - APPLIES TO A "DEFAULT JUDGMENT" OR A "DISMISSAL," NEITHER OF WHICH OCCURRED HERE. (SEE HOSSAIN V. HOSSAIN (2007) 157 CAL.APP.4TH 454, 459.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-21-2018 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANT ELISABETH THIERIOT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 25, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. RESPONDENT MS. THIERIOT MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL MOVING PAPERS BY TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 4, 2018. PETITIONER'S MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION BY TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 11, 2018. THE COURT NOTED THAT THE TWO QUESTIONS THAT SHOULD BE ADDRESSED ARE WHY THE JUDGMENT SHOULD BE VACATED AND WHY IT WOULD BE TIMELY TO DO SO NOW. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cynthia Castillo, Esq. 415-954-4400 of Farella Braun & Martel LLP for Petitioner Farella Braun & Martel.
Elisabeth Thieriot, Esq. 415-259-9900 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ELISABETH THIERIOT's Motion To Vacate Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Respondent Elisabeth Theriot's motion to vacate the judgment filed April 26, 2016 is denied. The motion is untimely per CCP 663a. Assuming without deciding that petitioner Farella Braun + Martel, LLP did not serve a required notice of arbitration as contended by Ms. Theriot, that is not a basis to avoid the strict time limits to seek to vacate a judgment per CCP 663a. The court's records reflect that within the time frame permitted to vacate the judgment Ms. Theriot represented by counsel filed both an application to stay execution of the judgment and a motion to set aside the judgment, showing that she had the ability to and could raise all arguments that she believed appropriate within the time frame of section 663a.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Elisabeth Thieriot, Petitioner, 415.259.9900, appearing as a self-represented litigant;
James Baker, Jr. of Farella Braun + Martel LLP, 415.954.4965, appearing for the Respondent.

Matter called, brief discussions are held with regard to the matter being continued and the parties filing supplemental papers.

At the conclusion of discussions, DEFENDANT ELISABETH THIERIOT's Motion To Vacate Judgment is continued to Tuesday, September 25, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. Respondent Ms. Thieriot may file supplemental moving papers by Tuesday, September 4, 2018. Petitioner's may file a supplemental opposition by Tuesday, September 11, 2018. The Court noted that the two questions that should be addressed are why the judgment should be vacated and why it would be timely to do so now.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Continued For Letter To Sep-22-16 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Order To Confirm Arbitration Award And Resulting Judgment In Favor Of Petitioner
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney,James Baker, Jr. of Farella Braun & Martel LLp (415-954-4400) appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party

Attorney, William Caspari of Caspari Law Office (510-316-9811) appears as counsel for respondent/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT ELISABETH THIERIOT'S Motion To Set Aside Order To Confirm Arbitration Award And Resulting Judgment In Favor Of Petitioner is DENIED for each of three independently sufficient reasons. First, the CCP 1288.2 100-day deadline for moving to vacate an arbitration award is mandatory and jurisdictional, so CCP 473(b) relief is not available. (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211-12.) Second, Theiriot did not retain the counsel who supposedly erred until after the 100-day deadline had passed, so his supposed error did not cause the deadline to be missed. Third, CCP 473(b)(2) - under which Theiriot moves - applies to a "default judgment" or a "dismissal," neither of which occurred here. (See Hossain v. Hossain (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 454, 459.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jul-15-2016 Continued To Aug-17-16 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Hearing On Farella Braun And Martel Llps Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anthony Schoenberg of Farella Braun + Martel LLP telephone number 415-954-4963 for Petitioner Farella Braun + Martel LLP.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PETITIONER FARELLA BRAUN + MARTEL LLP'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. Respondent Elisabeth Thieriot's only opposition to the motion is that there are grounds to vacate the arbitration award per CCP 1286.2. However, per CCP 1288.2 any request to vacate an arbitration award must be served and filed not later than 100 days after service of the award. Because Ms. Theriot did not serve and file a request to vacate the award within 100 days, this "court has no option but to confirm the award." (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal. App. 4th 1199, 1211-12). Ms. Theriot's argument that she may oppose a petition to confirm on the grounds that the award should be vacated after the 100 day period has expired as long as the opposition is filed within ten days of the petition is contrary to established law. (Eternity Investments, Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal. App. 4th 739). Order and separate judgment signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Nor reported. (302/HK)
